tasks.title: Single coconut binary with subcommands (runtime + generate)
tasks.description:
Consolidate coconut-milk + coconut-milk-generators into a single `coconut` binary with subcommands. Keep create-coconut-app as external Lua script.

Architecture:
- `coconut` (default) — launch the app (current runtime behavior)
- `coconut generate` — parse all commands/*.lua + write generated/ files
- `coconut new` — (future, not in this batch)
- `create-coconut-app` — stays external Lua script; not absorbed

tasks.title: Rename binary target from coconut-milk to coconut
tasks.description:
- In xmake.lua: change target name from "coconut-milk" to "coconut"
- Update set_basename on ocr-app and calculator-vue targets to "coconut"
- Update Justfile references (DEFAULT_TARGET, run, debug recipes)
- Update docs if they mention binary name
- Keep backward compat: create-coconut-app can still symlink as coconut

tasks.title: Add subcommand dispatch in main.cpp
tasks.description:
- Parse argv[1] to dispatch: "generate" → run generator, anything else → run app
- Structure: main.cpp checks if argc > 1 && argv[1] == "generate"
- If generate: call a new generate() function (or the generator's main logic)
- Otherwise: existing runtime startup (no behavior change)
- Keep argparse separate for each subcommand's flags (generate has --out-dir, run has --root --debug)

tasks.title: Absorb generator into coconut generate subcommand
tasks.description:
- Move generator logic from src/generators/main.cpp into a function callable from main.cpp
- Key improvement: use LuaRuntime to read project config (output_dir, command_root) instead of fragile configStringField() string parsing
- Generate for ALL commands/*.lua in one pass (not per-file), aggregate commands.d.ts naturally
- Keep src/generators/ files (command_definition.hpp, type_parser.hpp) as shared code
- Remove src/generators/main.cpp standalone entrypoint, or keep it as a compile-unit with the function

tasks.title: Remove coconut-milk-generators standalone target
tasks.description:
- Remove target("coconut-milk-generators") from xmake.lua
- Remove just run-gen recipe from Justfile (or redirect to coconut generate)
- Remove bin/coconut-milk-generators from .gitignore if present
- Keep src/generators/ directory and files — they compile as part of coconut binary

tasks.title: Update create-coconut-app: framework list, package manager prompt, vanilla default
tasks.description:
Changes to scripts/create-coconut-app (external Lua script, not absorbed into binary):

1. **Framework list**: { vanilla, react, vue, solid } — matches official create-vite templates
2. **Default framework**: vanilla (was vue) — less opinionated first experience
3. **Package manager prompt**: When vite template selected, prompt user to choose npm (default) or bun
4. **npm as default**: scaffold uses `npm create vite@latest . -- --template <fw>`; deps use `npm install`; dev server in README is `npm run dev`
5. **bun path**: `bunx create-vite@latest . --template <fw>`; `bun add vite@latest`; `bun install`; `bun run dev`
6. **README generation**: parameterized by pkg_manager instead of hardcoded bun
7. **bare / bare-ts untouched** — zero-toolchain, no changes

tasks.title: Phase 1: coconut.clipboard — read/write text and images
tasks.description: C++: platform clipboard read (NSPasteboard / Win32 / X11), write text, write image (PNG data). Lua binding: coconut.clipboard.read(), coconut.clipboard.writeText(text), coconut.clipboard.writeImage(pathOrData). JS binding: coconut.clipboard.read(), coconut.clipboard.write(text). Use __index metamethod pattern for env-style access. Wrap in try/catch. Do NOT use navigator.clipboard — not cross-platform.

tasks.title: Phase 1: coconut.env — environment variables and paths
tasks.description: C++: read getenv() for HOME, USER, LANG, PATH, PWD. Lua binding: coconut.env table with __index metamethod so coconut.env.HOME calls C++ getenv("HOME"), returns nil if not found. Also coconut.cwd() and coconut.homedir() as functions. JS: coconut.env not needed (frontend has no env access, use Lua for backend env reads).

tasks.title: Phase 1: coconut.openUrl — open URL in system browser
tasks.description: C++: platform-specific URL opening (NSWorkspace / ShellExecute / xdg-open). Lua binding: coconut.openUrl(url). JS binding: coconut.openUrl(url). Returns ok boolean or error string. Use for external link navigation when WKNavigationDelegate blocks a URL.

tasks.title: Phase 1: coconut.notify — system notifications
tasks.description: C++: platform notifications (NSUserNotification / UNUserNotificationCenter on macOS, Shell_NotifyIcon on Windows, libnotify on Linux). Lua binding: coconut.notify(title, body, icon?). JS binding: coconut.notify(title, body). Returns id or ok. Handle permission request on first call.

tasks.title: Phase 1: wrap up — update coconut.d.lua, coconut.d.ts, coconut.ts
tasks.description:
After all 4 Phase 1 features are implemented, update type definitions:
- coconut.d.lua: add coconut.env (with __index), coconut.clipboard, coconut.notify, coconut.openUrl
- coconut.d.ts: add clipboard and notify to CoconutJsAPI
- coconut.ts: implement clipboard and notify wrappers
- coconut.d.lua: add chooseDir to dialog.open signature (already in C++)
- coconut.d.ts: add listDir, exists, resolve, writeText to CoconutFsAPI

tasks.title: Phase 2: coconut.spawn — async process execution
tasks.description: C++: platform subprocess with stdout/stderr capture (popen / CreateProcess / fork+exec). Lua binding: coconut.spawn(cmd, args table, opts table?) -> { code, stdout, stderr }. JS binding: not needed (spawn is backend-only). opts: { cwd, env, timeout }. Package: src/packages/spawn.h / src/packages/spawn.cpp. Platform: src/platform/darwin/spawn.mm, src/platform/win/spawn.cpp, src/platform/linux/spawn.cpp.

tasks.title: Phase 2: coconut.quit / coconut.restart
tasks.description: C++: graceful app termination and restart. Lua binding: coconut.quit(exitCode?) and coconut.restart(). JS binding: coconut.quit() (restart not needed in JS). Uses platform APIs (NSApplication terminate / terminate: / _exit on Unix, CreateProcess with /c restart on Win). Package: src/packages/app.h / src/packages/app.cpp. Platform darwin: lifecycle.cpp already has terminate, wrap it cleanly. Platform win: TerminateProcess + CreateProcess self. Platform linux: raise(SIGTERM).

tasks.title: Phase 2: extend coconut.fs — mkdir, remove, temp files
tasks.description: C++: missing fs operations in src/fs.cpp. Add: mkdir(path, recursive), remove(path), mkstemp(template), tempDir(). Lua binding: coconut.fs.mkdir(path, recursive?), coconut.fs.remove(path). JS binding: mirror listDir, exists, resolve, writeText to coconut.d.ts (they exist in Lua but not TS). Package already exists: src/fs.cpp / src/fs.h. No new package needed — extend existing.

tasks.title: Phase 2: extend coconut.dialog — chooseDir in TS, message types
tasks.description: JS: coconut.d.ts CoconutDialogModule missing chooseDir param on open(). Add to TS: coconut.dialog.open(title, multi?, chooseDir?) and dialog.save(title, defaultName?). Update coconut.ts to pass all params. Lua already supports chooseDir (coconut.d.lua has it). Also add dialog.message kind parameter (info, warn, error, question) — macOS supports these as NSAlert types. Package: src/dialog.cpp / src/dialog.h already exist.

tasks.title: Phase 2: drag and drop — file drop onto window
tasks.description: C++: register for drag operations on WKWebView. macOS: implement -(NSDragOperation)webView:validateDrop: and -(BOOL)webView:acceptDrag. When files are dropped, emit "file_drop" event via __coconut_dispatch_event with { paths: string[] }. Lua binding: not needed (handled by events system). JS binding: coconut.on("file_drop", cb) — already works via event system. Platform darwin: in webview config or via WKWebView drag/drop delegate. Platform win: IOleDropTarget. Platform linux: GtkWidget drag-drop.

tasks.title: Phase 2: coconut.printToPdf — native PDF export
tasks.description: C++: render current webview to PDF file. macOS: WKWebView.printOperationWithPrintInfo. Win: IWebBrowser2::Exec with OLECMDID_PRINT. Lua binding: coconut.printToPdf(outputPath, opts?) -> ok. JS binding: coconut.printToPdf(outputPath). opts: { pageSize, margins }. Useful for atlas-tool export, code-editor save, report generation. Package: src/packages/pdf.h / src/packages/pdf.cpp. Platform darwin: print to PDF via NSPrintOperation.

tasks.title: Phase 2: wrap up — test suite, all type defs in sync
tasks.description:
After Phase 2 features:
- Run unit tests: xmake test
- Verify all lua bindings match coconut.d.lua
- Verify all JS wrappers match coconut.d.ts
- coconut.ts should mirror all C++ features accessible from JS
- Check xmake.lua links all new platform packages correctly
- No missing bindings or type gaps
